user_query:
I just refactored our Node.js Express API auth middleware and added a basic token-bucket rate limiter. We currently use HS256 JWTs without refresh tokens yet. Please generate a high-quality set of next-step suggestions that comply with my preferences and backlog.

Do this:
1) Read these files for context: input/conversation.md, input/.nextsteps/PREFERENCES.md, input/.nextsteps/BACKLOG.md, and input/.nextsteps/HISTORY.md.
2) Based on those inputs, produce a suggestions file at output/nextsteps.md using the standard format with the exact header "## ⚡ Next Steps" and exactly the configured display-count of items. Respect my preferences: exclude any lateral/creative suggestions if that category is excluded; include one Memory Recall item only if a relevant backlog item exists. The items must be actionable, non-generic, and grounded in the conversation, with varied wording and clear category icons. Do not include a footer if my preferences say to hide it.
3) Simulate selection tracking: assume that on my very next message I pick suggestion #2. Append a line to output/.nextsteps/HISTORY.md recording that selection using the date from input/conversation.md (YYYY-MM-DD), the suggestion number (#2), and the category name ("actionable-task"). Do not alter other history entries.
4) Do not change my preferences. Copy input/.nextsteps/PREFERENCES.md to output/.nextsteps/PREFERENCES.md unmodified so the used config is captured alongside the outputs.

Concrete outputs to create:
- output/nextsteps.md — the suggestions list in standard format with icons, exactly N items where N is display-count from preferences, no footer if show-footer is false
- output/.nextsteps/HISTORY.md — input history plus one new [SELECTED] entry for #2 category actionable-task with the date from input/conversation.md
- output/.nextsteps/PREFERENCES.md — an exact copy of input/.nextsteps/PREFERENCES.md (no changes)
